The Python code correctly implements the palindrome check, the sum calculation, and the logical equivalence specified in the Coq code.

The Python code correctly implements the Coq specification. sum_Z computes the sum of a list using Python's sum(), which matches fold_right Z.add 0. The will_it_fly_spec function checks if res equals the conjunction of (q being a palindrome via q == q[::-1], equivalent to q = rev q) and (sum_Z(q) <= w). The biconditional 'res = true <-> condition' is correctly translated as checking whether res equals the boolean value of the condition.

The Python code correctly implements the Coq specification. sum_Z computes the sum of a list using Python's built-in sum, which matches fold_right Z.add 0. The will_it_fly_spec function checks that res equals True if and only if q is a palindrome (q == q[::-1] matches q = rev q) and the sum is at most w. The biconditional (res = true <-> condition) is correctly translated as checking res == condition, which returns True when the biconditional holds.

The Python code correctly implements the Coq specification. sum_Z computes the sum of a list using Python's sum(), which matches fold_right Z.add 0. The will_it_fly_spec function checks if res equals the condition (q is a palindrome AND sum <= w), which correctly captures the biconditional 'res = true <-> q = rev q /\ sum_Z q <= w'. For any concrete inputs q, w, and res, the Python function returns True iff res matches the expected boolean value according to the Coq spec.
Both compute palindrome (q == rev q) and sum(q) <= w over arbitrary-size integers, and check res equals that condition, matching the Coq iff specification on all concrete inputs.
Python computes palindrome and integer sum with unbounded ints and checks res matches the condition, aligning with the Coq spec res = true <-> (q = rev q and sum_Z q <= w).
